message:
feat(remote): runtime-backed lifecycle observability, SpawnRejected error, explicit close()

Extend the RemoteRuntime trait and RemoteHandle with first-class
lifecycle state tracking so origin-side code can observe remote task
progression (Pending → Running → Completed/Cancelled/Failed) without
waiting for the terminal result.

RemoteRuntime trait additions:
- observe_task_state(): returns the last known lifecycle state for a
  remote task, allowing RemoteHandle::state() to query live state from
  the runtime rather than relying solely on the locally cached snapshot.
- clear_task_state(): cleans up runtime-tracked state after a terminal
  result is consumed locally, preventing unbounded state accumulation.
  Both methods have default no-op implementations for backward compat.

RemoteHandle changes:
- state() now returns RemoteTaskState by value (Copy) instead of &ref,
  and queries the attached runtime for live state when the handle hasn't
  yet reached a terminal condition.
- close(): new async method that sends a best-effort cancellation and
  then drains the remote lifecycle to a terminal state—the explicit
  cleanup counterpart to drop-based implicit cleanup.
- join(), try_join(): call clear_runtime_state() after consuming the
  terminal result so the runtime's pending map doesn't grow unbounded.
- RemoteHandle now carries an optional Arc<dyn RemoteRuntime> set at
  spawn time from the RemoteCap.

New error variant:
- RemoteError::SpawnRejected(SpawnRejectReason): surfaces explicit
  rejection from the remote node (e.g., capacity exceeded) as a
  distinct terminal error rather than conflating it with NodeDown or
  generic failures.

RemoteTaskState is now Copy (was Clone-only), simplifying pattern
matching and state comparisons throughout.

Tests:
- remote_handle_state_observes_runtime_lifecycle: verifies state()
  transitions through Pending → Running → Completed via runtime marks.
- remote_handle_close_sends_cancel_and_drains_runtime_result: verifies
  close() sends CancelRequest and clears runtime state.
- remote_handle_runtime_rejection_fails_closed: verifies SpawnRejected
  propagation.
- attached_remote_handle_observes_ack_before_result (E2E): verifies
  handle state transitions through the full harness with network sim.
- All existing tests updated for state()-by-value and runtime: None in
  manually constructed handles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
